title: Watching golf: majors, tours, and India's stars
microFact: Four men's majors: Masters, PGA Championship, US Open, The Open. Clubhouse small talk runs on them.
lesson:
Fluency in golf-as-spectacle is clubhouse currency. The essentials:

THE FOUR MEN'S MAJORS: the MASTERS (Augusta National, every April — the green jacket), the PGA CHAMPIONSHIP (May), the US OPEN (June — brutal setups), and THE OPEN CHAMPIONSHIP (July, links golf in the UK — the oldest, the Claret Jug). Winning all four in a career = the Grand Slam.

TOURS: the PGA TOUR (US) and DP WORLD TOUR (Europe) headline; LIV Golf is the breakaway everyone has opinions about — having a light take on it is practically a membership requirement. Women's golf runs the LPGA and five majors.

INDIA'S NAMES to know: Anirban Lahiri, Shubhankar Sharma, and Gaganjeet Bhullar internationally; ADITI ASHOK, India's biggest golf star, a Bangalorean who nearly medaled at the Tokyo Olympics; the domestic men's circuit is the PGTI (Professional Golf Tour of India). The HERO INDIAN OPEN at DLF G&CC in Gurugram is the country's flagship event.

Minimum viable fandom: watch the majors' final rounds (late Sunday night IST — the golfer's insomnia), and know who's currently world #1. Thirty minutes of highlights a month covers you.

quiz.question: Which of these is NOT one of the four men's majors?
quiz.explanation: The Players is huge ('the fifth major') but not a major. Masters, PGA Championship, US Open, The Open — that's the slam.
